title: Bermudiana hartwegii
intro: Bermudiana hartwegii, also known as the Bermuda buttonwood or Bermuda cedar, is a type of deciduous shrub native to the Caribbean and southern Florida. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, likely due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bermudiana hartwegii include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Bermudiana hartwegii is often prepared as a decoction or tea, although it may also be used in the form of a tincture or infused oil.

Bermudiana hartwegii is a member of the genus Bermudiana, which contains a small number of species.
